sections: Canonical artifacts, renderer-derived views, and Projections
HCM-0.6 exact shipped kind and root-profile data
HCM-2.1 Project Context canonical-YAML pilot proof gate
First-party intake, renderers, and Projection posture
complete selected dependency and transition record

repo_truth: Fresh read-only Review 2 of the exact nine-entry remediated HCM-2.1 planning subject. Review 1 returned six Required findings over the original seven-entry manifest; the parent validated all six against live source and recorded bounded docs-only remediation without product edits. Independently verify that the exact remediated packet closes the Environment Inventory legacy-reference dependency, one-observation doctor/race contract, deterministic YAML/Markdown byte algorithms, flow DTO/budget/freshness/log contract, non-Unix mutation boundary, and premature-review-status issue without introducing new contradictions or forbidden scope. Inherit neither Review 1 findings nor the parent remediation as correct without live replay.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay the sorted nine-entry manifest and aggregate fingerprint before review.
Review every byte of 00, 04, 06, HCM-2.1 SPEC, plan, unchecked todo, both planning proof files, and immutable Review 1 dispatch.
Read Review 1 cited live engine/compiler/flow/CLI/template/fixture/test sources and unchanged 01, 02, 03, 05, 07, 08, HCM-0.6 decision, selected HCM-1.4 record, and definition assets only as needed.
Run bounded read-only Markdown, link, Git, handoff/schema, source/API feasibility, DTO/byte-contract, cross-platform, compatibility, determinism, security, and proof checks.
Return findings first with exact file/line evidence through the built-in channel.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user task hops.
No Rust, Cargo, definition, template, fixture, test, runtime/product, pipeline, HCM-2.2+, SDK, Tauri, Substrate, contract, dock, or publication implementation.

Do not invent an alternative remediation, schema field, compatibility mode, serializer, renderer rule, observation model, DTO, budget domain, platform guarantee, Environment Inventory conversion, or pipeline migration.
Do not treat Review 1, the remediation proof, passing old baseline tests, or contingent control-pack wording as evidence that the remediated packet is clean.
tasks: Invoke required skills in order, remain read-only, replay the exact nine-entry manifest/aggregate, and return BLOCKED if any byte differs.
Review findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order with severity, exact file/line, violated contract/gate, concrete failure, smallest valid remediation, and missing/invalidated proof.

Revalidate all six Review 1 findings against live source and verify each remediation is total, internally consistent, implementable inside the newly enumerated files, and reflected across SPEC, plan, todo, 00, 04, 06, and proof.

Verify Environment Inventory changes are reference-only yet remove every live legacy Project Context dependency needed by authoring/template/fixture/all-three smoke without silently converting its authority.

Verify the retained observation, reason precedence, final identity/byte stability, typed/render failure mapping, and doctor projection cannot combine observations or emit stale fingerprints and have runnable substitution/identical-byte ABA proof.

Verify the closed YAML scalar/framing algorithm, exhaustive Markdown transform, literal fixtures, typed registry invocation, and independent fingerprints leave no byte-significant implementation choice.

Verify exact owned-path DTO fields/order, section mode, source/render domains, effective budget thresholds, manifest/freshness inputs, fixture lineage, logs, compiler JSON/shared/inspect, and CLI output are complete and do not break unchanged siblings.

Verify the non-Unix fail-before-mutation contract is implementable without editing the fallback writer, preserves exact author/doctor/flow error semantics, and names honest cross-compile plus native Windows proof/stop behavior.

Verify outcome-neutral status wording, docs-only current scope, unchecked todo, bridge admission/deletion, classification ceiling, review/remediation lineage, staged detection, and two-commit parent closeout remain exact.
Return CLEAN only if there is no valid Critical or Required finding; otherwise return CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits and use bounded checks rather than indefinite waits.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest/aggregate, required authority, live source, or mandatory read-only verification cannot be reproduced.

Return CHANGES_REQUIRED rather than selecting unspecified bytes, DTO behavior, observation semantics, Environment Inventory conversion, unsafe platform fallback, compatibility, pipeline, or later-slice behavior.
Return to the parent if valid remediation would require implementation now, a schema/kind/profile/descriptor change, broader product authority, HCM-2.2+, or global handoff/ledger mutation.
